title: Dental Hygiene * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Dental Hygiene * Veterans 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Disabilities Issues Native American Community Older Adults Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF DENTAL LIFELINE NETWORK DONATED DENTAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive dental services to older adults, people with disabilities, and medically fragile individuals through a network of volunteer dentists and dental labs. A volunteer dentist will come up with a treatment plan, with the goal of making sure the patient is pain-free and able to eat properly. Does not provide emergency care. Services may include: -Dental cleanings -Cavity prevention (sealants, fluoride treatments/supplements) -Dental prosthodontics (dentures, bridges, crowns, implants) -Orthodontics -Endodontics -Dental restoration -Periodontics -Oral/maxillofacial surgery -Cosmetic dentistry 1800 15th Street Suite 100 Denver, CO 80202 Eligibility: Veterans and individuals who cannot pay for dental care and who are age 65 and older, have a permanent disability, or are medically compromised. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (503) 594-0837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OREGON VETERAN DENTAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides dental care such as teeth cleanings, filling, extractions, emergency dental care, and some dentures. 500 Summer Street NE E-49 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Veterans who live in Oregon and do not qualify for full OHP benefits and have household income at or below 400% federal poverty level. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (800) 699-9075 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DIVISION OF MEDICAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS OREGON VETERAN DENTAL PROGRAM
